open source · MIT

demo videos
in one command

Turn any URL into a production-grade demo video. Capture, narrate, render — all in pure Python. No bundling, no Chromium, no hassle.

Get started See why
$dvg make-video https://your-app.com # 15 seconds, 1080p, mixed audio

What it does

A four-stage deterministic pipeline. Every stage is testable, every artifact is schema-validated.

●

Capture

Playwright-driven 1080p capture with full DOM event log. Auto-pilot scenarios scroll, click, and tour your page.

●

Analyze

Events become scenes and anchors. Deterministic when DOM events exist; visual gap-filler when they don't.

●

Direct

One brain picks the soundtrack, places captions, sets the mood. LLM-ready interface; ships with a heuristic.

Why dvg

Faster than Remotion. Cleaner than a manual editor. Designed for engineers who ship.

▲

One language

Pure Python. No Node, no React, no webpack bundle step. Composition is a Pydantic model.

♪

Audio-first

−14 LUFS YouTube target enforced. Sidechain ducking under captions. Production-grade by default.

⚡

Render fast

libass + ffmpeg, single-shot. No Chromium per render. 12s output renders in 3 seconds.

Render time, 12s output

3.0s

dvg — single ffmpeg invocation

Webpack bundle alone

~25s

Remotion — before any rendering starts

Get started

$pip install dvg
$dvg make-video https://your-app.com
→ final.mp4 (1080p, −14 LUFS, 12s)
dvg · MIT licensed · made with love and ffmpeg
